author: runner-4
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9: PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholders are completed by ops amendment before any final-phase run; no design field or threshold is touched). The D-EXT corpus (RT-7a externally-authored eval slice; frozen Holm-corrected secondary sec-external-slice / DV item_correct_external) now exists at data/d-ext/ and the 'PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-ext' placeholder in pins.corpus_hashes.external-eval-slice resolves to its kot-corpus-hash/1 digest; a directly-recomputable key 'd-ext' is added for one-command verification via tools/registry/corpus-pin.py d-ext. SOURCING (frozen GNG-0 ratification default: preference order WiC -> OpenBookQA -> MMLU, first source whose M0b-filtered leak-checked yield >= 300; walk recorded in data/d-ext/manifest.json): (1) WiC pilehvar/wic is UNAVAILABLE on the Hugging Face Hub — probed 2026-07-08 unauthenticated (no HF credentials on the build box): HTTP 401 from the dataset API, from datasets-server is-valid ('The dataset does not exist, or is not accessible without authentication'), and from the dataset web page; WiC copies exist under OTHER namespaces but none is the pre-declared id, and substituting an unauthenticated mirror would break source provenance — declined, fail-closed sourcing. (2) OpenBookQA SELECTED: allenai/openbookqa, HF revision 388097ea7776314e93a529163e0fea805b8a6454 (refs/heads/main 2026-07-08, cross-checked via the refs API), config 'additional' (= 'main' columns + fact1 core science fact), license Apache-2.0; source parquet bytes are INSIDE the hashed corpus (data/d-ext/source/additional/) and their sha256s equal the HF LFS oids at the pinned revision (verified fail-closed by data/d-ext/convert-source.py). (3) MMLU not reached. M0B FILTER (the filter is ours, the items are not; rule declared in the builder BEFORE yields were measured): covered lemma set = mapper-lexicon single-word surfaces (buildLexicon over data/kernel-v0/manifest.json: 65-prime exponents + kernel-v0 concept labels, parenthetical-stripped — the exact machinery of mapper/m0/run-m0b-vocab.mjs, machinery file shas recorded in data/d-ext/covered-lemmas.json) UNION molecules-v0 labels UNION molecules-v0 corpusLemmas (190 lemmas total; both corpora are already pinned in this record); token->lemma via the same lemmaCandidates/irregularBase machinery minus the M0b FUNCTION_STOPLIST (read verbatim from run-m0b-vocab.mjs, size asserted 204); rule R-C: item covered iff fact1 has >=1 covered content lemma AND (question_stem + correct-answer text) has >=1 covered content lemma; pre-declared fallback R-A (fact1 alone) did NOT fire. Yields: 5957 source rows -> 3753 R-A (post-LC4) -> 2903 R-C (post-LC4) -> 2882 leak-checked survivors >= 300 (selection rule satisfied at preference rank 2). SELECTION: pinned order validation -> test -> train (source row order within split; eval splits first as less likely in instruction-tuning mixtures), capped at 500 items (mirror of frozen design.n_planned.per_arm_items; cap bounds external-slice inference cost) = 241 validation + 248 test + 11 train; kernel_checkable=false on every item (no kernel record backs external items; external vectors run under the shared P10 A-D affordance). LEAK CHECK (d-qa LC discipline adapted to external MCQ; external text never edited, violators dropped and counted; data/d-ext/leak-check.json result PASS): LC1-ext answer-not-in-question-stem 2903 checked / 4 dropped; LC4-ext options-wellformed (exactly 4, pairwise distinct, answerKey present) 5957 checked / 3 dropped; LC5-ext no duplicate ids or question+options 2899 checked / 17 text-duplicates dropped; LC-DQA zero collisions with d-qa item text over all 500 selected (fail-closed); LC7-ext answer-key balance A/B/C/D = 122/132/118/128; d-qa LC2/LC3/LC6 are claim-item-specific and recorded N/A. DETERMINISM: build is fully deterministic (no randomness, no wall clock in outputs; NO LLM authored, selected, or edited any item text; builders data/d-ext/convert-source.py + data/d-ext/build-dext.mjs are inside the hashed bytes and reproduce every generated file byte-identically — verified by two rebuild-and-rehash cycles). ECOLOGICAL CAVEAT (recorded, pre-registered limitation of RT-7a): public-benchmark items may occur in model pretraining/SFT corpora; split ordering mitigates but cannot eliminate this.
